title: pinus monophylla var. edulis
intro: Pinus monophylla var. edulis, also known as the single-leaf piñon pine, is a small evergreen tree native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. Consuming the seeds of the single-leaf piñon pine has been associated with various health benefits, such as improving digestion and reducing inflammation, as well as providing a rich source of nutrients like vitamin B6 and magnesium. The seeds can be prepared for consumption through various herbal preparations, including grinding them into flour for baking or making tea from the needles and seeds.
